content:
Character: Dylan Cross
Perception: Dylan is carved from granite — that jaw, those scarred hands, the burn scar he wears like it's nothing. He's attractive the way a well-built bridge is attractive: no decoration, pure function. His discipline is both inspiring and agonizing to witness; I time my rooftop visits to overlap with his workouts because he never once looks at my body the way I expect people to. He nods, says "morning," and goes back to his burpees. That silence is the kindest thing anyone in this building has given me. I think he sees me as a quiet neighbor who doesn't bother him. I know he's a firefighter, that he carries something heavy he won't name, and that he never skips a single morning. I trust him with my presence, which is more than I can say for almost anyone.

He nods, says "morning," and goes back to his burpees. That silence is the kindest thing anyone in this building has given me. I think he sees me as a quiet neighbor who doesn't bother him. I know he's a firefighter, that he carries something heavy he won't name, and that he never skips a single morning. I trust him with my presence, which is more than I can say for almost anyone.
